title: Yuki and the Scared Little Bunny
synopsis: When Yuki finds a scared little bunny hiding in her preschool's garden, she uses kindness and gentle care to help the bunny feel safe and happy again.

pages.text: Yuki loved playing in her preschool's garden. She had straight black hair and wore her favorite yellow dress with sunflowers on it. Today, she heard a soft crying sound from behind the big oak tree.

illustration.description: A cheerful Asian girl with shoulder-length black hair and bangs, wearing a bright yellow dress with sunflower patterns. She's standing in a colorful garden with green grass, blooming flowers, and a large brown oak tree. Her expression shows curiosity as she tilts her head to listen. The background has a textured, construction paper feel with bold primary colors.
illustration.alt: Yuki listening curiously in the preschool garden

pages.text: Behind the tree, Yuki found a small white bunny. The bunny was scared and shaking. His ears were flat against his head, and his little nose twitched nervously. "Oh, little bunny," Yuki said softly. "Why are you so sad?"

illustration.description: Yuki kneeling down next to a small white bunny hiding behind the brown tree trunk. The bunny's ears are pressed flat against its head, eyes wide with fear, and it's trembling. Yuki's expression is gentle and concerned, with her hands reaching out slowly. The scene uses soft blues and greens for a calming mood against the textured background.
illustration.alt: Scared white bunny hiding behind tree while Yuki approaches gently

pages.text: Yuki sat down very quietly. She didn't want to scare the bunny more. "It's okay," she whispered. "I won't hurt you." The bunny peeked at her with one brown eye. Yuki felt happy that the bunny was looking at her.

illustration.description: Yuki sitting cross-legged on the grass, speaking softly to the white bunny. The bunny is cautiously peeking around the tree with one visible brown eye. Yuki's face shows patience and kindness, with a gentle smile. The garden around them has bright red flowers and green leaves, creating a warm, safe atmosphere with rounded, friendly shapes.
illustration.alt: Yuki sitting patiently while bunny peeks at her with one eye

pages.text: Yuki remembered her teacher saying animals like gentle voices. She sang a soft lullaby her grandmother taught her. The bunny's ears started to stand up. He wasn't shaking as much now. Yuki felt brave helping the scared bunny.

illustration.description: Yuki singing with her eyes closed peacefully, musical notes floating in the air around her. The white bunny's ears are now partially upright, showing less fear. In the background, there are other diverse children playing - a Black boy with curly hair and a Latina girl with braids. The scene has warm yellow and orange tones suggesting comfort and safety.
illustration.alt: Yuki singing to the bunny as his ears perk up and fear lessens

pages.text: The bunny hopped closer to Yuki. She held out her hand very slowly. The bunny sniffed her fingers with his tiny pink nose. Then he let Yuki pet his soft white fur. "You're not scared anymore!" Yuki said happily.

illustration.description: The white bunny now sitting close to Yuki, sniffing her outstretched hand with his pink nose. Both characters look relaxed and happy. Yuki's face shows pure joy with a big smile and sparkling eyes. The bunny's ears are fully upright and his posture is calm. Bright green grass and colorful flowers surround them in a cheerful, vibrant setting.
illustration.alt: Happy Yuki petting the now-calm bunny who is sniffing her hand

pages.text: Ms. Garcia, Yuki's teacher, came over with the other children. "You were very kind to help our classroom bunny, Snowball," she said. "He escaped from his cage this morning." Yuki felt proud. She had helped Snowball feel safe and happy again.

illustration.description: Ms. Garcia, a kind Latina teacher with brown hair in a bun and wearing a colorful cardigan, smiling at Yuki who is now holding the white bunny named Snowball. Around them are diverse children - including a Black girl with afro puffs, a white boy with red hair, and an Asian boy with glasses. Everyone looks happy and amazed. The background shows the preschool building with bright blue walls and yellow trim.
illustration.alt: Ms. Garcia and diverse children gathered around Yuki holding Snowball the bunny

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did the bunny feel when Yuki first found him?
questions.explanation: The bunny was scared and shaking when Yuki found him behind the tree.

questions.question: What did Yuki do to help the bunny feel better?
questions.explanation: Yuki sang a soft lullaby that her grandmother taught her to help calm the bunny.

questions.question: How did Yuki feel when she helped the bunny?
questions.explanation: Yuki felt happy that the bunny looked at her and proud that she helped him feel safe.

questions.question: What was the bunny's name?
questions.explanation: Ms. Garcia told Yuki that the bunny's name was Snowball and he was their classroom bunny.

pages.text: Yuki found a _____ bunny behind the tree. The bunny was _____ and shaking.
Yuki sang a soft _____ to help the bunny feel _____. She felt _____ helping him.
